justification: Bap1+/- knockout mice chronically exposed to asbestos showed significantly higher mesothelioma incidence than WT; all four criteria met.
Nf2+/- knockout mice treated with asbestos showed accelerated mesothelioma formation versus WT; genetic susceptibility outcome directly measured.
Heterozygous p53+/- mice given crocidolite asbestos showed increased mesothelioma incidence and reduced latency versus WT.
Blm+/- mice injected with asbestos had shorter survival and higher mesothelioma incidence versus controls; genetic susceptibility measured.
NLRP3-deficient mice given asbestos compared to WT for mesothelioma incidence and survival; genetic modification's susceptibility effect studied.
Asbestos-exposed NF2+/- versus NF2+/-CR-/- mice compared for mesothelioma survival; calretinin knockout susceptibility effect assessed despite biomarker focus.
Fixed Nf2+/-;Cdkn2a+/- genotype used as background to test drug chemoprevention; no genotype comparison studying genetic effect on susceptibility.
Study profiles gene expression differences between MexTAg and WT mesotheliomas; mesothelioma susceptibility (incidence/latency/burden) by genotype not the measured outcome.
Dietary antioxidant intervention in fixed-genotype MexTAg mice; no genetic modification whose effect on susceptibility is studied.
p53+/- mice with crocidolite asbestos positive control developed mesothelioma; asbestos arm in genetic model cannot be confidently ruled out.
Ribozyme therapy study on human mesothelioma cell line and xenografts; no asbestos administered and no mouse genetic susceptibility tested.
No abstract; title on asbestos inhalation carcinogenesis is field-relevant and cannot be ruled out per conservative rule.
In-vitro biochemical study of scavenger-receptor binding to asbestos in transfected CHO cells; no mouse mesothelioma outcome.
Study conducted in rats with chrysotile and radon; no mice and no mesotheliomas occurred.
In-vitro study of calretinin knockout mouse primary mesothelial cell proliferation and migration; no asbestos exposure or in-vivo mesothelioma outcome.
Nanotube/asbestos mesothelioma study in rats and mice examining immune cells; species/strain differences, no defined genetic modification studied.
